item.title: AI as Your Creative Partner
item.dc:creator: admin
item.excerpt:encoded: Using AI to amplify human creativity, not replace it.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Using AI to amplify human creativity, not replace it. A 5-minute discussion starter.
a:2:{i:0;s:45:"Display or share screen for a short prompt";i:1;s:70:"Optional: one device with an AI image or text tool for a quick demo";}

a:2:{i:0;a:3:{s:4:"term";s:13:"Generative AI";s:10:"definition";s:150:"AI that creates new content (text, images, music, code) based on patterns in training data. It recombines existing patterns rather than having original ideas.";s:18:"key_stage_adapted";b:0;}i:1;a:3:{s:4:"term";s:30:"Authenticity (in creative work)";s:10:"definition";s:161:"The quality of being genuine and original — reflecting your own voice, experiences, and creative choices rather than copying or delegating to others.";s:18:"key_stage_adapted";b:0;}}

a:2:{i:0;a:2:{s:9:"objective";s:66:"Recognise that AI can support creative work without replacing human ideas";s:10:"assessable";b:1;}i:1;a:2:{s:9:"objective";s:85:"Understand that the best results often come from human direction plus AI assistance";s:10:"assessable";b:1;}}

a:3:{i:0;a:6:{s:4:"step";i:1;s:6:"action";s:88:"Ask: "When you use AI to make something — a story, picture, or song — who do you think is the creator?"";s:8:"duration";s:5:"1 min";s:12:"resource_ref";s:0:"";s:13:"student_action";s:23:"Quick think or pair share";s:11:"teacher_tip";s:50:"Accept a range of answers; you will refine in step 3.";}i:1;a:6:{s:4:"step";i:2;s:6:"action";s:102:"Share a simple example: e.g. "I gave the AI a idea and it made a picture. Was the idea or the picture more important?"";s:8:"duration";s:5:"2 min";s:12:"resource_ref";s:0:"";s:13:"student_action";s:11:"Discussion";s:11:"teacher_tip";s:0:"";}i:2;a:6:{s:4:"step";i:3;s:6:"action";s:108:"Summarise: "AI is a creative partner. You bring the idea and the choices; AI helps you explore. The human is still in charge."";s:8:"duration";s:5:"1 min";s:12:"resource_ref";s:0:"";s:13:"student_action";s:0:"";s:11:"teacher_tip";s:0:"";}}
When you use AI to create something, who do you think is the real creator — you or the AI?
a:2:{i:0;s:66:"What would be boring or wrong if we let AI do everything without our input?";i:1;s:52:"How can you stay "in charge" when using AI for a project?";}

a:6:{i:0;s:99:"It depends on HOW AI was used and how much of YOUR creative input and decision-making was involved.";i:1;s:95:"Using AI for brainstorming or starting points is often still your work — like using a dictionary or thesaurus.";i:2;s:88:"Letting AI generate almost the entire piece with minimal editing means it is much less clearly "yours".";i:3;s:70:"Key question: can you explain and defend every creative choice in the final work?";i:4;s:99:"Ask yourself: would you be comfortable if a teacher or employer knew exactly how AI was used in this piece?";i:5;s:120:"The human brings vision, judgement, emotional truth, personal perspective, and the final decisions — AI follows instructions.";}

Students may worry that AI "takes over" creativity. Emphasise that AI is a tool that responds to human prompts and choices. Good analogy: like a brush for a painter — the brush does not decide the painting. Encourage examples from their own use of AI (e.g. image generators, chatbots) and link to the idea of staying critical and in control.

a:3:{s:7:"support";s:95:"Use a single, concrete example (e.g. one image prompt) and keep the discussion to "who had the idea?".";s:6:"stretch";s:120:"Ask students to design a short "human + AI" creative project and say what they would do vs what they would ask AI to do.";s:4:"send";s:99:"Pre-teach "creator" and "partner". Use visual cues (e.g. you + robot icon) to show human in charge.";}
a:1:{i:0;a:2:{s:8:"activity";s:78:"Try one small creative task with an AI tool and note what you decided vs what the AI did";s:4:"type";s:8:"homework";}}
